title: Addition & Subtraction Basics
description: Learn to add and subtract numbers with fun problems for young learners

questions.text: What is 2 + 3?
hints: Count on your fingers starting from 2
Hold up 2 fingers, then 3 more fingers
questions.explanation: 2 + 3 = 5. If you hold up 2 fingers on one hand and 3 on the other, you have 5 fingers total.

questions.text: What is 1 + 4?
questions.explanation: 1 + 4 = 5. Starting from 1, count four more: 2, 3, 4, 5.

questions.text: What is 3 + 3?
hints: Think of a double: the same number added to itself
Hold up 3 fingers on each hand
questions.explanation: 3 + 3 = 6. When you add a number to itself, it is called a double. Double 3 is 6.

questions.text: What is 7 - 2?
hints: Start at 7 and count backwards 2 times
questions.explanation: 7 - 2 = 5. Starting from 7, count back two: 6, 5.

questions.text: What is 5 - 1?
hints: If you have 5 toys and give away 1, how many are left?
questions.explanation: 5 - 1 = 4. If you take away 1 from 5, you are left with 4.

questions.text: What is 4 + 2?
hints: Start at 4 and count up 2 more
Think of 4 cats and 2 more cats joining them
questions.explanation: 4 + 2 = 6. Starting at 4, count two more: 5, 6.

questions.text: What is 6 + 3?
hints: Use a number line to hop forward 3 from 6
questions.explanation: 6 + 3 = 9. Starting at 6, count three more: 7, 8, 9.

questions.text: What is 8 - 3?
questions.explanation: 8 - 3 = 5. Starting at 8, count back three: 7, 6, 5.

questions.text: What is 9 - 4?
hints: Start at 9 and count back 4 times
questions.explanation: 9 - 4 = 5. Starting at 9, count back four: 8, 7, 6, 5.

questions.text: What is 0 + 5?
hints: Zero plus any number equals that number
questions.explanation: 0 + 5 = 5. When you add zero to any number, the answer is that number itself.

questions.text: What is 7 + 5?
hints: Break 5 into 3 and 2 to make a 10 first
questions.explanation: 7 + 5 = 12. You can break 5 into 3 + 2. First add 7 + 3 = 10, then 10 + 2 = 12.

questions.text: What is 8 + 6?
questions.explanation: 8 + 6 = 14. Break 6 into 2 + 4. First 8 + 2 = 10, then 10 + 4 = 14.

questions.text: Sam has 6 stickers. He gets 4 more. How many stickers does Sam have now?
questions.explanation: 6 + 4 = 10. Sam started with 6 stickers and got 4 more, so he now has 10 stickers.

questions.text: Mia has 9 crayons. She gives away 3. How many crayons does Mia have left?
questions.explanation: 9 - 3 = 6. Mia started with 9 crayons and gave away 3, so she has 6 left.

questions.text: What is 15 - 7?
questions.explanation: 15 - 7 = 8. You can subtract 5 first to get 10, then subtract 2 more to get 8.

questions.text: What number goes in the box? 3 + ___ = 7
hints: What do you add to 3 to make 7?
questions.explanation: 3 + 4 = 7. Counting up from 3 to 7 takes 4 steps: 4, 5, 6, 7.

questions.text: What number goes in the box? ___ + 5 = 9
hints: Try 9 - 5 to find the missing number
questions.explanation: 4 + 5 = 9. You can find the missing number by subtracting: 9 - 5 = 4.

questions.text: What is 11 - 6?
questions.explanation: 11 - 6 = 5. Subtract 1 first to get 10, then subtract 5 more to get 5.

questions.text: There are 5 birds in a tree. 3 more birds come. Then 2 fly away. How many birds are in the tree?
questions.explanation: 5 + 3 = 8, then 8 - 2 = 6. First the birds increased to 8, then 2 flew away leaving 6.

questions.text: What number goes in the box? 10 - ___ = 6
hints: What do you subtract from 10 to get 6?
questions.explanation: 10 - 4 = 6. The difference between 10 and 6 is 4.

questions.text: 5 + 5 = 10. True or false?
questions.explanation: True! 5 + 5 = 10. This is a doubles fact. Five plus five equals ten.

questions.text: 9 - 5 = 3. True or false?
questions.explanation: False! 9 - 5 = 4, not 3. Count back from 9 five times: 8, 7, 6, 5, 4.

questions.text: Adding zero to a number gives you the same number. True or false?
questions.explanation: True! Adding zero to any number gives you that same number. For example, 7 + 0 = 7.

questions.text: 6 + 7 = 12. True or false?
hints: Try adding carefully: 6 + 6 = 12, so 6 + 7 is one more
questions.explanation: False! 6 + 7 = 13, not 12. Since 6 + 6 = 12, adding one more gives 13.

questions.text: If you subtract a number from itself, you always get zero. True or false?
hints: If you have some toys and give them ALL away, how many are left?
questions.explanation: True! Any number minus itself equals zero. For example, 8 - 8 = 0 and 3 - 3 = 0.

questions.text: What is 9 + 8?
hints: Make a ten first by breaking 8 into 1 and 7

questions.text: What is 16 - 9?
Tom had some marbles. He lost 4 and now has 8 left. How many marbles did Tom start with?
hints: Add the ones he lost back: 8 + 4
questions.explanation: 8 + 4 = 12. Tom has 8 now, and he lost 4, so he started with 8 + 4 = 12 marbles.

questions.text: What number goes in the box? ___ - 6 = 8
hints: Add 6 and 8 together to find the missing number
questions.explanation: 14 - 6 = 8. To find the missing number, add: 8 + 6 = 14.

title: Shapes & Their Properties
description: Discover circles, triangles, squares, and rectangles and learn about their sides and corners

questions.text: How many sides does a triangle have?
hints: Think of a shape that looks like a slice of pizza
questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides. The prefix 'tri' means three.

questions.text: How many sides does a square have?
hints: Think of a window or a tile on the floor
All sides of this shape are the same length
questions.explanation: A square has 4 sides, and all 4 sides are the same length.

questions.text: Which shape has NO sides and NO corners?
hints: Think of a ball or the sun
questions.explanation: A circle has no sides and no corners. It is perfectly round.

questions.text: How many corners does a rectangle have?
hints: A rectangle is like a stretched square
Count the pointy parts where two sides meet
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 4 corners. Each corner is where two sides meet.

questions.text: Which shape looks like a door?
hints: A door is taller than it is wide
It has 4 sides but is not a square
questions.explanation: A door is shaped like a rectangle. It has 4 sides, with two longer sides and two shorter sides.

questions.text: What shape is a wheel?
hints: It is round with no corners
questions.explanation: A wheel is a circle. Circles are round and can roll because they have no corners.

questions.text: How many corners does a triangle have?
hints: A triangle has the same number of corners as sides
questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 corners. Each corner is where two sides meet.

questions.text: Which shape has 4 equal sides?
hints: All four sides are the same length
questions.explanation: A square has 4 sides that are all the same length.

questions.text: What shape is a slice of pizza?
questions.explanation: A slice of pizza is shaped like a triangle. It has 3 sides and a pointy tip.

questions.text: Which shape is round?
hints: Think of the shape of the sun
questions.explanation: A circle is the round shape. It has no straight sides or corners.

questions.text: A rectangle has 2 long sides and 2 short sides. How many sides does it have in all?
hints: Add the long sides and the short sides together
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 4 sides in all: 2 long sides + 2 short sides = 4 sides.

questions.text: Which shape has more sides: a triangle or a square?
questions.explanation: A square has more sides. A square has 4 sides and a triangle has 3 sides. 4 is more than 3.

questions.text: How are a square and a rectangle alike?
hints: Count the sides on each shape
Think about what they have in common
questions.explanation: Both a square and a rectangle have 4 sides and 4 corners. A square is actually a special kind of rectangle.

questions.text: If you put two triangles together, what shape can you make?
hints: Think about cutting a square diagonally
Two right triangles can fit together
questions.explanation: When you put two triangles together, you can make a square or a rectangle.

questions.text: Which real-life object is shaped like a rectangle?
hints: Think about flat objects with 4 sides
It has two longer sides and two shorter sides
questions.explanation: A book is shaped like a rectangle. It has 4 sides with two longer sides and two shorter sides.

questions.text: How many corners does a circle have?
hints: Corners are pointy places where sides meet
A circle is smooth all around
questions.explanation: A circle has 0 corners. It is perfectly round with no pointy parts.

questions.text: I have 3 corners and 3 sides. What shape am I?
hints: The number 3 is a big clue
questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 corners and 3 sides. The clue is the number 3.

questions.text: Which of these shapes can roll?
hints: To roll, a shape needs to be round
Corners would stop a shape from rolling smoothly
questions.explanation: A circle can roll because it is round with no corners. Shapes with corners cannot roll smoothly.

questions.text: A stop sign has 8 sides. Does it have more or fewer sides than a square?

choices: A stop sign has no sides

questions.explanation: A stop sign has more sides. It has 8 sides and a square has only 4 sides. 8 is more than 4.

questions.text: What shape is the face of a clock?
hints: Think about a round clock on the wall
The hands spin around in a round shape
questions.explanation: Most clocks have a circular face. The circle shape lets the hands go around smoothly.

questions.text: A square has 4 sides. True or false?
hints: Count the sides of a square
questions.explanation: True! A square has exactly 4 sides, and all 4 sides are equal in length.

questions.text: A circle has 4 corners. True or false?
hints: Think about the shape of a ball
Are there any pointy parts on a circle?
questions.explanation: False! A circle has 0 corners. It is round and smooth with no pointy parts.

questions.text: A triangle has more sides than a rectangle. True or false?
questions.explanation: False! A triangle has 3 sides and a rectangle has 4 sides. 3 is less than 4.

questions.text: All squares are also rectangles. True or false?
hints: A rectangle has 4 sides and 4 corners
A square also has 4 sides and 4 corners
questions.explanation: True! A square is a special rectangle where all 4 sides are the same length.

questions.text: A shape with 3 straight sides is always called a triangle. True or false?
hints: What is the only shape with exactly 3 sides?
questions.explanation: True! Any shape with exactly 3 straight sides is a triangle, no matter how it looks.

questions.text: A square has sides that are each 3 blocks long. If you walked all around the square, how many blocks would you walk?
questions.explanation: 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 12 blocks. A square has 4 equal sides, so you add the length 4 times.

questions.text: How many triangles can you see in a rectangle that has a line drawn from one corner to the opposite corner?
hints: Each half of the rectangle becomes a triangle
questions.explanation: You can see 2 triangles. The diagonal line splits the rectangle into two equal triangles.

questions.text: Which shape has the fewest number of sides?
hints: A circle has no straight sides at all
Among shapes with sides, which has the fewest?
questions.explanation: The triangle has the fewest sides with 3. A circle has no straight sides, but among shapes with sides, the triangle has the least.

title: Place Value & Comparison
description: Understand tens and ones, compare numbers, and learn expanded form

questions.text: In the number 34, how many tens are there?
hints: The tens digit is on the left
The first digit tells you how many groups of ten
questions.explanation: In 34, the digit 3 is in the tens place, so there are 3 tens (which equals 30).

questions.text: In the number 56, how many ones are there?
hints: The ones digit is on the right
questions.explanation: In 56, the digit 6 is in the ones place, so there are 6 ones.

questions.text: Which number is greater: 25 or 19?
questions.explanation: 25 is greater than 19. 25 has 2 tens and 19 has 1 ten. Since 2 tens is more, 25 is greater.

questions.text: What number is made of 4 tens and 2 ones?
hints: Add the ones to the tens: 40 + 2
questions.explanation: 4 tens = 40 and 2 ones = 2. Put them together: 40 + 2 = 42.

questions.text: Which symbol goes in the blank? 15 ___ 22
hints: The open side of the symbol faces the bigger number
questions.explanation: 15 < 22. Fifteen is less than twenty-two. The < symbol means 'less than.'

questions.text: How many ones are in 1 ten?
hints: Count from 1 to see how many make a ten
Think of a group of ten objects
questions.explanation: 1 ten = 10 ones. A group of ten ones makes one ten.

questions.text: What number comes right after 49?
hints: After 49 comes a new group of tens
questions.explanation: The number right after 49 is 50. When you count up 1 from 49, you get 50.

questions.text: Which number is the smallest: 38, 83, or 33?
hints: 3 tens and 3 ones is the smallest
questions.explanation: 33 is the smallest. 33 has 3 tens and 3 ones, 38 has 3 tens and 8 ones, 83 has 8 tens and 3 ones.

questions.text: In the number 70, how many ones are there?
hints: Look at the digit in the ones place
The 0 on the right tells you the ones
questions.explanation: In 70, there are 0 ones. The digit in the ones place is 0.

questions.text: Which is greater: 47 or 74?
questions.explanation: 74 is greater. 74 has 7 tens while 47 has only 4 tens. Even though they use the same digits, their positions matter.

questions.text: What is 30 + 7 in standard form?
hints: Just combine the tens and ones
questions.explanation: 30 + 7 = 37. This is called expanded form: 37 broken into its tens (30) and ones (7).

questions.text: What is the expanded form of 65?
hints: Break the number into tens and ones
6 is in the tens place, so it stands for 60
questions.explanation: 65 = 60 + 5. The 6 in the tens place means 60, and the 5 in the ones place means 5.

questions.text: Put these numbers in order from least to greatest: 45, 29, 61
questions.explanation: 29, 45, 61. From least to greatest: 29 (2 tens), then 45 (4 tens), then 61 (6 tens).

questions.text: Which number has 5 in the tens place?
hints: The tens place is the left digit in a two-digit number
Look for a number where 5 is the first digit
questions.explanation: 52 has 5 in the tens place. In 15 and 25, the 5 is in the ones place. In 55, the 5 is in both places, but 52 most clearly shows 5 in the tens place.

questions.text: What is 40 + 16?
hints: 40 is 4 tens, 16 is 1 ten and 6 ones
questions.explanation: 40 + 16 = 56. Add the tens: 4 tens + 1 ten = 5 tens (50). Add the ones: 6. So 50 + 6 = 56.

questions.text: Which symbol makes this true? 58 ___ 58
questions.explanation: 58 = 58. When two numbers are exactly the same, we use the equals sign.

questions.text: If you have 7 tens, what number do you have?
questions.explanation: 7 tens = 70. Each ten equals 10, so 7 tens = 7 x 10 = 70.

questions.text: What number is 10 more than 36?
hints: The tens digit goes up by 1
questions.explanation: 10 more than 36 is 46. Adding 10 increases the tens digit by 1: 3 tens becomes 4 tens.

questions.text: Put these numbers in order from greatest to least: 72, 27, 77
hints: 77 has the most tens and ones combined
questions.explanation: 77, 72, 27. From greatest to least: 77 is biggest, then 72, then 27 is smallest.

questions.text: What is the expanded form of 90?
hints: Write it as tens plus ones
questions.explanation: 90 = 90 + 0. There are 9 tens (90) and 0 ones, so the expanded form is 90 + 0.

questions.text: The number 52 has more tens than the number 25. True or false?
hints: Look at the tens digit in each number
questions.explanation: True! 52 has 5 tens and 25 has 2 tens. 5 is more than 2.

questions.text: The number 99 is the largest two-digit number. True or false?
hints: 9 is the biggest digit, and 99 has 9 in both places
questions.explanation: True! 99 is the largest two-digit number. It has the biggest digit (9) in both the tens and ones places.

questions.text: 46 is greater than 64. True or false?
questions.explanation: False! 46 has 4 tens and 64 has 6 tens. Since 4 < 6, 46 is less than 64.

questions.text: 20 + 8 = 28 is an example of expanded form. True or false?
hints: Expanded form shows the value of each digit
20 is the value of the tens, 8 is the value of the ones
questions.explanation: True! 20 + 8 = 28 shows the number broken into its tens value (20) and ones value (8).

questions.text: A number with 6 tens is always greater than a number with 5 tens. True or false?
hints: 6 tens means the number is at least 60
5 tens means the number is at most 59
questions.explanation: True! A number with 6 tens is 60-69, and a number with 5 tens is 50-59. Every number from 60-69 is greater than every number from 50-59.

questions.text: Maya says 3 tens and 15 ones equals 45. Is she correct?
hints: You can have more than 9 ones and regroup
questions.explanation: Yes! 3 tens = 30 and 15 ones = 15. So 30 + 15 = 45. Even though 15 ones is more than 9, you can still add them.

questions.text: Which number is exactly halfway between 30 and 50?
hints: Halfway means the same distance from both numbers
What is the middle of 30 and 50?
questions.explanation: 40 is exactly halfway between 30 and 50. It is 10 away from both 30 and 50.

questions.text: I am a two-digit number. My tens digit is 3 more than my ones digit. My ones digit is 2. What number am I?
questions.explanation: The number is 52. The ones digit is 2, and the tens digit is 2 + 3 = 5. So the number is 52.

title: Symmetry & Spatial Sense
description: Explore mirror images, symmetry lines, and how shapes look from different views

questions.text: What is a line of symmetry?

choices: A line that makes a shape bigger
A line that splits a shape into two matching halves
A line that goes around a shape
A line that connects two shapes

hints: Think of folding a shape in half
Both halves should look the same
questions.explanation: A line of symmetry splits a shape into two halves that are mirror images of each other. If you fold along the line, both sides match up perfectly.

questions.text: Which letter has a line of symmetry: A, F, G, or J?
hints: Imagine folding each letter in half
One of these letters looks the same on both sides
questions.explanation: The letter A has a vertical line of symmetry. If you draw a line down the middle, both sides match.

questions.text: If you look in a mirror, your reflection is a ___.
hints: What do we call what we see in a mirror?
It looks like you but flipped
questions.explanation: Your reflection in a mirror is called a mirror image. It looks like you, but left and right are swapped.

questions.text: Does a circle have a line of symmetry?
hints: Think about folding a circle in half
You can fold it many different ways and it still matches
questions.explanation: A circle has many (infinite) lines of symmetry! You can fold it in half along any line through the center and both halves match.

questions.text: Which of these shapes is symmetrical?
hints: Which shape has two matching halves?
Think about folding each one down the middle
questions.explanation: A heart shape is symmetrical. If you draw a line down the middle of a heart, both sides match.

questions.text: If you fold a square in half from top to bottom, what shape do you get?
hints: Folding a square in half makes it shorter but the same width
The top half covers the bottom half
questions.explanation: Folding a square in half from top to bottom gives you a rectangle. It has the same width but half the height.

questions.text: Which shape has exactly 4 lines of symmetry?
hints: This shape has all equal sides
You can fold it 4 different ways and both halves match
questions.explanation: A square has exactly 4 lines of symmetry: one vertical, one horizontal, and two diagonal.

questions.text: What does a mirror image of the letter b look like?
hints: Imagine holding the letter b up to a mirror
The mirror flips left to right
questions.explanation: The mirror image of b is d. When you flip b left to right, the bump switches from the right side to the left side, making d.

questions.text: If you stand facing a mirror and raise your right hand, which hand does your reflection raise?
hints: A mirror flips things left and right
Try it with a real mirror!
questions.explanation: Your reflection raises its left hand. A mirror swaps left and right, so your right appears as left in the reflection.

questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a rectangle (that is not a square) have?
hints: You can fold a rectangle in half two ways
Think about folding it left-right and top-bottom
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry: one vertical (left-right) and one horizontal (top-bottom).

questions.text: A butterfly has its wings spread out. How many lines of symmetry does it have?
hints: Look at a butterfly from above
The left wing matches the right wing
questions.explanation: A butterfly has 1 line of symmetry running down the middle of its body. The left wing is a mirror image of the right wing.

questions.text: If half of a symmetrical shape looks like a hill, what does the whole shape look like?

choices: A hill and its mirror image

hints: Imagine flipping the hill to the other side
questions.explanation: The whole shape looks like a hill and its mirror image. Symmetry means you reflect one half to create the other half.

questions.text: Which capital letter does NOT have a line of symmetry?
hints: Try folding each letter in half
One of these letters has no way to fold it into matching halves
questions.explanation: P does not have a line of symmetry. O, T, and X all have at least one line of symmetry, but P cannot be folded into matching halves.

questions.text: If you cut a pizza in half with one straight cut through the center, are both halves symmetrical?

choices: Only if the cut goes through the center
Only if the pizza is square

hints: The cut must go through the exact center
questions.explanation: Both halves are symmetrical only if the cut goes through the center of the pizza. A cut through the center of a circle creates two matching halves.

questions.text: If you place a mirror on the line of symmetry of the letter M, what will you see?
hints: The mirror shows the reflection of one half
questions.explanation: You will see the full letter M. The mirror reflects the half you can see, and since M is symmetrical, the reflection completes the letter.

questions.text: If you turn a square a quarter turn (90 degrees), does it look the same?
hints: Think about what happens when you rotate a square
All sides of a square are the same
questions.explanation: Yes! When you turn a square a quarter turn, it looks exactly the same because all four sides are equal and all four corners are the same.

questions.text: Which shape would look different if you flipped it upside down?
hints: Think about shapes with a top and bottom that look different
Which shape has a pointy top?
questions.explanation: A triangle looks different when flipped upside down. It goes from pointing up to pointing down. Circles, squares, and rectangles look the same when flipped.

questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does an equilateral triangle (all sides equal) have?
hints: Each line goes from a corner to the middle of the opposite side
An equilateral triangle has 3 equal sides
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry. Each line goes from one corner to the middle of the opposite side.

questions.text: If you look at the word MOM in a mirror, what does it still spell?
hints: M and O are symmetrical letters
Think about how each letter looks when flipped
questions.explanation: MOM still spells MOM in a mirror! The letters M and O are symmetrical, so they look the same when reflected.

questions.text: Where would you draw a line of symmetry on a smiley face?

choices: Down the middle of the nose (vertical)
From one ear to the other (diagonal)
A smiley face has no symmetry

hints: The left side of a face matches the right side
Draw the line from top to bottom through the center
questions.explanation: A vertical line down the middle of the nose divides a smiley face into two matching halves. The left eye matches the right eye, and the smile is split evenly.

questions.text: A square has more lines of symmetry than a rectangle. True or false?
hints: A square has 4 lines of symmetry
A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry
questions.explanation: True! A square has 4 lines of symmetry (vertical, horizontal, and two diagonals) while a rectangle has only 2 (vertical and horizontal).

questions.text: A mirror image is always the same size as the original. True or false?
hints: Think about your reflection in a flat mirror
Does the mirror make things bigger or smaller?
questions.explanation: True! A mirror image in a flat mirror is always the same size as the original. It is just flipped left to right.

questions.text: The letter Z has a line of symmetry. True or false?
hints: Try folding Z in half different ways
questions.explanation: False! The letter Z does not have a line of symmetry. No matter how you try to fold it, the two halves do not match.

questions.text: If a shape has a line of symmetry, folding it along that line makes both halves match. True or false?
hints: This is the definition of symmetry
Think about folding a paper heart in half
questions.explanation: True! That is exactly what a line of symmetry does. When you fold along the line, both halves overlap perfectly.

questions.text: A shape can have more than one line of symmetry. True or false?
hints: Can you fold it in more than one way?
questions.explanation: True! Many shapes have more than one line of symmetry. A square has 4 and a circle has many.

title: Multiplication Mastery
description: Master multiplication through times tables, properties, and real-world word problems

questions.text: What is 3 x 4?
questions.explanation: 3 x 4 means 3 groups of 4. Counting by 4 three times: 4, 8, 12. So 3 x 4 = 12.

questions.text: What is 5 x 2?
questions.explanation: 5 x 2 means 5 groups of 2. That is 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 10.

questions.text: What is 6 x 3?
questions.explanation: 6 x 3 = 18. You can count by 3 six times to get the answer.

questions.text: What is 7 x 2?
questions.explanation: 7 x 2 = 14. Multiplying by 2 is the same as doubling: 7 + 7 = 14.

questions.text: 4 x 5 equals 20.
questions.explanation: 4 x 5 = 20. Counting by 5 four times gives 5, 10, 15, 20.

questions.text: What is 9 x 3?
hints: Use the trick: 10 x 3 = 30, then subtract 3.
questions.explanation: 9 x 3 = 27. A handy trick: 10 x 3 = 30, and 30 - 3 = 27.

questions.text: What is 8 x 4?
questions.explanation: 8 x 4 = 32. You can think of it as doubling twice: 8 x 2 = 16, then 16 x 2 = 32.

questions.text: 2 x 10 equals 20.
hints: 2 with a zero at the end.
questions.explanation: 2 x 10 = 20. When you multiply any number by 10, you add a zero to the end.

questions.text: What is 5 x 5?
questions.explanation: 5 x 5 = 25. Counting by 5 five times: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25.

questions.text: What is 4 x 3?
questions.explanation: 4 x 3 = 12. Four groups of 3: 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 12.

questions.text: If 6 x 7 = 42, what is 7 x 6?
hints: The order of multiplication does not change the result.
This is called the commutative property.
questions.explanation: 7 x 6 = 42, the same as 6 x 7. The commutative property says that changing the order of the numbers does not change the product.

questions.text: 5 x 8 gives the same answer as 8 x 5.
hints: Does switching the order change the product?
questions.explanation: Yes! 5 x 8 = 40 and 8 x 5 = 40. The commutative property of multiplication tells us that the order does not matter.

questions.text: Which number sentence shows the commutative property?
hints: The commutative property swaps the order of the numbers.
Look for the one that shows the same multiplication in two orders.
questions.explanation: 3 x 4 = 4 x 3 shows the commutative property because the two numbers are swapped but the product stays the same.

questions.text: A baker makes 6 trays of cookies. Each tray has 8 cookies. How many cookies did the baker make?
hints: You need to multiply the number of trays by cookies per tray.
questions.explanation: 6 trays x 8 cookies per tray = 48 cookies in total.

questions.text: There are 7 bags with 5 apples in each bag. How many apples are there altogether?
hints: Multiply the number of bags by apples in each bag.
questions.explanation: 7 bags x 5 apples = 35 apples altogether.

questions.text: To find 6 x 14, you can break it into 6 x 10 + 6 x 4. What is 6 x 14?
hints: Then find 6 x 4 = 24. Now add them.
questions.explanation: Using the distributive property: 6 x 14 = 6 x 10 + 6 x 4 = 60 + 24 = 84.

questions.text: What is 4 x 13 using the distributive property (4 x 10 + 4 x 3)?
A classroom has 4 rows of desks with 9 desks in each row. How many desks are there?
hints: Multiply rows by desks per row.
questions.explanation: 4 rows x 9 desks = 36 desks in the classroom.

questions.text: You can use the distributive property to break 7 x 12 into 7 x 10 + 7 x 2.
hints: The distributive property lets you split one factor into parts.
questions.explanation: Correct! The distributive property allows us to break apart a factor: 7 x 12 = 7 x (10 + 2) = 7 x 10 + 7 x 2 = 70 + 14 = 84.

questions.text: 9 x 4 = 36. Using the commutative property, which is also true?
hints: The commutative property swaps the numbers being multiplied.
questions.explanation: By the commutative property, 4 x 9 = 36, the same as 9 x 4.

questions.text: A farmer plants 8 rows of corn with 12 plants in each row. Using the distributive property, how many plants are there?
hints: 8 x 10 = 80 and 8 x 2 = 16. Add them together.

questions.text: Sophia has 3 boxes. Each box has 4 bags, and each bag has 2 marbles. How many marbles does Sophia have?
hints: First find how many bags: 3 x 4.
Then multiply by 2 marbles per bag.
questions.explanation: 3 boxes x 4 bags = 12 bags. 12 bags x 2 marbles = 24 marbles in total.

questions.text: Which expression is equal to 5 x 18?
hints: Apply the distributive property: multiply 5 by each part.

questions.text: A school has 9 classrooms. Each classroom has 3 tables, and each table seats 4 students. How many students can sit in the school?
hints: Then multiply tables by students per table.
questions.explanation: 9 classrooms x 3 tables = 27 tables. 27 tables x 4 students = 108 students.

questions.text: Alex says 7 x 15 = 7 x 10 + 7 x 5. Ben says 7 x 15 = 7 x 20 - 7 x 5. Who is correct?
questions.explanation: Both are correct! Alex uses 15 = 10 + 5 and Ben uses 15 = 20 - 5. Both give 7 x 15 = 105.

title: Perimeter & Area Basics
description: Learn to calculate perimeter and area of rectangles and simple shapes

questions.text: What is the perimeter of a rectangle with length 5 cm and width 3 cm?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2 x (5 + 3) = 2 x 8 = 16 cm.

questions.text: What is the perimeter of a square with sides of 4 cm?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides. Perimeter = 4 x 4 = 16 cm.

questions.text: What is the area of a rectangle that is 6 cm long and 2 cm wide?
questions.explanation: Area = length x width = 6 x 2 = 12 square centimeters.

questions.text: The perimeter of a shape is the distance around it.
hints: Think about walking around the outside of a shape.
Perimeter measures the total length of all sides.
questions.explanation: True! The perimeter is the total distance around the outside of a shape, found by adding up all the side lengths.

questions.text: What is the area of a square with sides of 5 cm?
hints: Area of a square = side x side.
questions.explanation: Area = 5 x 5 = 25 square centimeters.

questions.text: Area is measured in square units.
hints: Area measures the space inside a flat shape.
We count how many unit squares fit inside.
questions.explanation: True! Area measures the space inside a shape and is always measured in square units (like square centimeters or square meters).

questions.text: What is the perimeter of a rectangle with length 8 cm and width 4 cm?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2 x (8 + 4) = 2 x 12 = 24 cm.

questions.text: What is the area of a rectangle that is 7 cm long and 3 cm wide?
questions.explanation: Area = 7 x 3 = 21 square centimeters.

questions.text: A rectangle has a length of 10 cm and width of 1 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2 x (10 + 1) = 2 x 11 = 22 cm.

questions.text: A rectangle has a length of 3 cm and width of 3 cm. What is its area?
hints: This rectangle is actually a square!
questions.explanation: Area = 3 x 3 = 9 square centimeters. A rectangle with equal sides is a square!

questions.text: A garden is 9 meters long and 5 meters wide. How much fencing is needed to go around it?
hints: Fencing goes around the garden, so find the perimeter.
questions.explanation: Fencing = perimeter = 2 x (9 + 5) = 2 x 14 = 28 meters.

questions.text: A rug covers a floor area of 8 m long and 6 m wide. What is the area of the rug?
questions.explanation: Area = 8 x 6 = 48 square meters.

questions.text: An L-shaped figure is made of two rectangles. One is 4 cm by 2 cm and the other is 3 cm by 2 cm. What is the total area?
hints: Find the area of each rectangle separately.
questions.explanation: Area of first rectangle = 4 x 2 = 8 sq cm. Area of second = 3 x 2 = 6 sq cm. Total = 8 + 6 = 14 sq cm.

questions.text: A rectangle has a perimeter of 20 cm and a length of 7 cm. What is its width?
questions.explanation: 20 = 2 x (7 + width), so 7 + width = 10, and width = 3 cm.

questions.text: Two rectangles can have the same perimeter but different areas.
hints: Think about a 1 x 5 rectangle and a 2 x 4 rectangle.
Do they have the same perimeter? The same area?
questions.explanation: True! A 1 x 5 rectangle has perimeter 12 and area 5. A 2 x 4 rectangle also has perimeter 12 but area 8. Same perimeter, different area.

questions.text: A playground is shaped like a rectangle that is 12 m long and 8 m wide. What is the area?
questions.explanation: Area = 12 x 8 = 96 square meters.

questions.text: A T-shaped figure can be split into two rectangles: one is 6 cm by 1 cm and the other is 2 cm by 3 cm. What is the total area?
A rectangle has an area of 24 sq cm and a length of 8 cm. What is its width?
questions.explanation: 24 = 8 x width, so width = 24 / 8 = 3 cm.

questions.text: A rectangle with sides 6 cm and 4 cm has a greater area than a square with sides 5 cm.
questions.explanation: False! Rectangle area = 6 x 4 = 24 sq cm. Square area = 5 x 5 = 25 sq cm. The square has a greater area.

questions.text: Tom wants to put a border around a poster that is 11 cm long and 7 cm wide. How long is the border?
hints: A border goes around the poster, so find the perimeter.
questions.explanation: Border length = perimeter = 2 x (11 + 7) = 2 x 18 = 36 cm.

questions.text: An irregular shape is made by cutting a 2 cm x 2 cm square from the corner of a 6 cm x 4 cm rectangle. What is the area of the remaining shape?
hints: Find the area of the big rectangle first.
Then subtract the area of the small square.
questions.explanation: Big rectangle area = 6 x 4 = 24 sq cm. Small square area = 2 x 2 = 4 sq cm. Remaining = 24 - 4 = 20 sq cm.

questions.text: A rectangular park has a perimeter of 30 m. If the length is 10 m, what is the area of the park?
hints: First find the width using the perimeter formula.
30 = 2 x (10 + width), so width = 5. Then find area.
questions.explanation: 30 = 2 x (10 + width), so 15 = 10 + width, width = 5 m. Area = 10 x 5 = 50 sq m.

questions.text: A shape is made of 3 identical squares, each with side 3 cm, placed in a row. What is the perimeter of the combined shape?
hints: The combined shape is a rectangle 9 cm by 3 cm.
Find the perimeter of this rectangle.
questions.explanation: Three squares in a row make a 9 cm x 3 cm rectangle. Perimeter = 2 x (9 + 3) = 2 x 12 = 24 cm.

questions.text: Mia has 20 meters of ribbon. She wants to make the largest possible square frame. What will be the area inside the frame?
hints: The ribbon forms the perimeter of the square.
Perimeter = 20 m, so each side = 20 / 4 = 5 m.
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 20 m. A square has 4 equal sides, so each side = 20 / 4 = 5 m. Area = 5 x 5 = 25 sq m.

questions.text: An L-shaped room is made of a 5 m x 4 m rectangle joined to a 3 m x 2 m rectangle. What is the perimeter of the L-shape if no sides overlap inside?
hints: Draw the L-shape and label all outer sides.
Count each outer edge carefully; some inner edges do not count.
questions.explanation: The L-shape outer edges are: 5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 2 + 2 + (the connecting segments). Carefully tracing the outside: 5, 2, 3, 2, 2, 4 going around gives a total of 5 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 2 + 4 + 2 + 4 = 24 m (depending on how the rectangles attach, the perimeter is 24 m).

title: Fractions Fundamentals
description: Understand fraction concepts, find equivalent fractions, compare and add fractions

questions.text: What fraction of a pizza is shaded if 3 out of 8 equal slices are shaded?
hints: The numerator is the number of shaded parts.
The denominator is the total number of equal parts.
questions.explanation: 3 shaded slices out of 8 total slices = 3/8.

questions.text: The fraction 1/2 means one part out of two equal parts.
hints: The top number tells how many parts we have.
The bottom number tells how many equal parts the whole is divided into.
questions.explanation: True! In the fraction 1/2, the numerator (1) tells us we have one part, and the denominator (2) tells us the whole is divided into 2 equal parts.

questions.text: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
hints: Multiply the numerator and denominator of 1/2 by the same number.
questions.explanation: 1/2 = 2/4. Multiplying both the numerator and denominator by 2 gives an equivalent fraction.

questions.text: What is the numerator in the fraction 5/9?
hints: The numerator is the top number.
It tells how many parts we have.
questions.explanation: In 5/9, the numerator is 5 (the top number). It tells us we have 5 parts out of 9.

questions.text: Which is greater: 1/3 or 1/5?
hints: When numerators are the same, the smaller denominator means a bigger piece.
Cutting into 3 parts gives bigger pieces than cutting into 5 parts.
questions.explanation: 1/3 > 1/5. When the numerator is the same, the fraction with the smaller denominator is larger because each piece is bigger.

questions.text: What is 1/4 + 1/4?
hints: When denominators are the same, add the numerators.
questions.explanation: 1/4 + 1/4 = 2/4 = 1/2. When denominators match, add the numerators: 1 + 1 = 2, giving 2/4 which simplifies to 1/2.

questions.text: 3/4 is greater than 2/4.
hints: Both fractions have the same denominator.
questions.explanation: True! When fractions have the same denominator, the one with the larger numerator is greater. 3 > 2, so 3/4 > 2/4.

questions.text: Which fraction is equivalent to 2/3?
hints: Multiply numerator and denominator by the same number.
questions.explanation: 2/3 = 4/6. Multiply both parts by 2: (2 x 2)/(3 x 2) = 4/6.

questions.text: What fraction of a group of 10 stars is 4 stars?
hints: Put the part over the whole.
questions.explanation: 4 out of 10 = 4/10, which can also be simplified to 2/5.

questions.text: What is 3/8 + 2/8?
hints: Same denominator, so add the numerators.
questions.explanation: 3/8 + 2/8 = 5/8. When denominators are the same, just add numerators: 3 + 2 = 5.

questions.text: Which fraction is equivalent to 3/5?
hints: Multiply numerator and denominator by the same number.

questions.text: Which is greater: 3/4 or 5/8?
questions.explanation: Convert 3/4 to eighths: 3/4 = 6/8. Since 6/8 > 5/8, we know 3/4 > 5/8.

questions.text: What is 2/5 + 1/5?
questions.explanation: 2/5 + 1/5 = 3/5. Same denominator, so add numerators: 2 + 1 = 3.

questions.text: Simplify the fraction 6/8.
hints: Find a number that divides both 6 and 8.
questions.explanation: 6/8 simplified: divide numerator and denominator by 2. 6 / 2 = 3, 8 / 2 = 4. So 6/8 = 3/4.

questions.text: 2/6 is equivalent to 1/3.
questions.explanation: True! 2/6 = 1/3. Dividing both numerator and denominator by 2 gives 1/3.

questions.text: Sam ate 2/6 of a cake and Lily ate 3/6 of the same cake. How much did they eat together?
questions.explanation: 2/6 + 3/6 = 5/6. They ate 5/6 of the cake together.

questions.text: Put these fractions in order from smallest to largest: 1/2, 1/4, 3/4.
hints: Convert to the same denominator or think of parts of a whole.
1/4 is one quarter, 1/2 is two quarters, 3/4 is three quarters.
questions.explanation: In order: 1/4 (smallest), then 1/2 (= 2/4), then 3/4 (largest). So 1/4, 1/2, 3/4.

questions.text: What is 1/3 + 1/6?
questions.explanation: Convert 1/3 to sixths: 1/3 = 2/6. Then 2/6 + 1/6 = 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.text: 5/5 is equal to 1 whole.
hints: When the numerator equals the denominator, you have all the parts.
5 out of 5 parts = the whole thing.
questions.explanation: True! When the numerator and denominator are equal, the fraction equals 1 whole. 5/5 = 1.

questions.text: Which fraction is NOT equivalent to 1/2?
hints: Check each: multiply 1/2 to see if you get each fraction.
questions.explanation: 3/8 is NOT equivalent to 1/2. The others all simplify to 1/2: 2/4 = 1/2, 3/6 = 1/2, 4/8 = 1/2. But 3/8 does not.

questions.text: A rope is 3/4 meter long. Another rope is 5/8 meter long. Which rope is longer, and by how much?
hints: Now compare 6/8 and 5/8, and find the difference.
questions.explanation: 3/4 = 6/8. The first rope (6/8) is longer than the second (5/8) by 6/8 - 5/8 = 1/8 meter.

questions.text: What is 1/4 + 2/3?
hints: Find a common denominator for 4 and 3. LCD = 12.
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 12. 1/4 = 3/12 and 2/3 = 8/12. So 3/12 + 8/12 = 11/12.

questions.text: Maya drank 1/3 of a juice bottle on Monday and 1/4 on Tuesday. What fraction of the bottle did she drink in total?
hints: Find a common denominator for 3 and 4.

questions.text: Which list shows fractions in order from greatest to least: 7/10, 3/5, 1/2?
hints: Now order from greatest to least.
questions.explanation: Converting to tenths: 7/10, 6/10, 5/10. Greatest to least: 7/10, 3/5 (6/10), 1/2 (5/10).

questions.text: Two pizzas are the same size. One is cut into 6 slices and the other into 8 slices. If you eat 4 slices from the first pizza and 5 slices from the second, from which pizza did you eat more?
hints: From the first: 4/6 = 2/3. From the second: 5/8.
questions.explanation: First pizza: 4/6 = 2/3 = 16/24. Second pizza: 5/8 = 15/24. Since 16/24 > 15/24, you ate more from the first pizza.

title: Factors & Multiples
description: Explore factor pairs, common factors, multiples, and learn about LCM and GCD

questions.text: Which of the following is a factor of 12?
hints: A factor divides the number evenly with no remainder.
questions.explanation: 4 is a factor of 12 because 12 / 4 = 3 with no remainder.

questions.text: What are all the factor pairs of 10?
hints: Find pairs of numbers that multiply to give 10.
questions.explanation: The factor pairs of 10 are (1, 10) and (2, 5). These are all the pairs of whole numbers that multiply to 10.

questions.text: 15 is a multiple of 5.
hints: A multiple of 5 is a number you get by multiplying 5 by a whole number.
questions.explanation: True! 15 = 5 x 3, so 15 is a multiple of 5.

questions.text: Which of the following is a multiple of 6?
hints: Which answer appears in that list?
questions.explanation: 24 is a multiple of 6 because 6 x 4 = 24.

questions.text: List all the factors of 8.
questions.explanation: The factors of 8 are 1, 2, 4, and 8. These are all the numbers that divide 8 with no remainder.

questions.text: Every number is a factor of itself.
hints: Can any number be divided by itself?
Any number divided by itself equals 1.
questions.explanation: True! Any number divided by itself equals 1, so every number is a factor of itself.

questions.text: What is the first multiple of 7 that is greater than 20?
hints: Which is the first one greater than 20?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 7: 7, 14, 21, 28... The first one greater than 20 is 21 (7 x 3).

questions.text: Which number is a common factor of 12 and 8?
hints: A common factor divides both numbers evenly.
questions.explanation: 4 is a common factor of 12 and 8. It divides both evenly: 12 / 4 = 3 and 8 / 4 = 2.

questions.text: What are the first five multiples of 4?
questions.explanation: The first five multiples of 4 are: 4 x 1 = 4, 4 x 2 = 8, 4 x 3 = 12, 4 x 4 = 16, 4 x 5 = 20.

questions.text: 1 is a factor of every number.
hints: Can every number be divided by 1?
Any number divided by 1 equals itself.
questions.explanation: True! 1 divides every number evenly, so 1 is a factor of every number.

questions.text: What is the greatest common factor (GCF) of 12 and 18?
hints: List factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. Find the largest common one.
questions.explanation: Factors of 12: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12. Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. Common factors: 1, 2, 3, 6. The greatest is 6.

questions.text: What is the least common multiple (LCM) of 4 and 6?
hints: List multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18... Find the smallest number in both lists.
questions.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16... Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18... The smallest common multiple is 12.

questions.text: What are all the common factors of 16 and 24?
hints: Factors of 24: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 24. Find what's in both.

questions.text: Which number is a common multiple of 3 and 5?
hints: A common multiple must be divisible by both 3 and 5.
Check: is 15 / 3 a whole number? Is 15 / 5?
questions.explanation: 15 is a common multiple of 3 and 5 because 15 / 3 = 5 and 15 / 5 = 3.

questions.text: The GCF of 9 and 15 is 3.
hints: Factors of 15: 1, 3, 5, 15. What is the greatest common one?
questions.explanation: True! Factors of 9: 1, 3, 9. Factors of 15: 1, 3, 5, 15. Common factors: 1, 3. The greatest is 3.

questions.text: What is the LCM of 3 and 7?
hints: 3 and 7 share no common factors other than 1.
When two numbers share no common factors, their LCM is their product.
questions.explanation: Since 3 and 7 are both prime and share no factors, LCM = 3 x 7 = 21.

questions.text: A number is a factor of 20 and also a factor of 30. Which of these could it be?
hints: Find factors of 30: 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15, 30. What's in both?
questions.explanation: 10 is a factor of both 20 (20/10 = 2) and 30 (30/10 = 3).

questions.text: How many factors does 18 have?
hints: List all factors: start with 1 and 18.
questions.explanation: Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. That is 6 factors.

questions.text: The LCM of 6 and 8 is 48.
hints: List multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24... What is the smallest common one?
questions.explanation: False! Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18, 24... Multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24... The LCM is 24, not 48.

questions.text: Two bells ring together at noon. One rings every 6 minutes, the other every 8 minutes. After how many minutes will they ring together again?
questions.explanation: The bells ring together at the LCM of 6 and 8. Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18, 24... Multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24... LCM = 24 minutes.

questions.text: What is the GCF of 24 and 36?
hints: Factors of 36: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 12, 18, 36. Find the greatest common one.

questions.text: Maria has 18 red beads and 24 blue beads. She wants to make identical groups using all beads, with no beads left over. What is the greatest number of groups she can make?
hints: She needs groups that evenly divide both 18 and 24.
questions.explanation: GCF of 18 and 24 = 6. Maria can make 6 groups (each with 3 red and 4 blue beads).

questions.text: What is the LCM of 5 and 12?
hints: 5 and 12 share no common factors besides 1.
questions.explanation: Since 5 and 12 share no common factors (besides 1), LCM = 5 x 12 = 60.

questions.text: A teacher wants to arrange 36 students into equal rows. Which of the following is NOT a possible number of rows?
hints: The number of rows must be a factor of 36.
Check: is 36 divisible by 4? By 6? By 8? By 9?
questions.explanation: 36 / 4 = 9, 36 / 6 = 6, 36 / 9 = 4, but 36 / 8 = 4.5 (not a whole number). 8 is NOT a factor of 36.

questions.text: Hot dogs come in packs of 8 and buns come in packs of 6. What is the smallest number of each you need to buy so there are no leftovers?
hints: Find the LCM of 8 and 6 to know the smallest equal number.
questions.explanation: LCM of 8 and 6 = 24. You need 24 hot dogs (3 packs of 8) and 24 buns (4 packs of 6) for no leftovers.

title: Fraction Operations
description: Master adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing fractions including mixed numbers and unlike denominators.

questions.text: What is 1/4 + 1/4?
hints: The denominators are the same, so just add the numerators.
questions.explanation: 1/4 + 1/4 = 2/4 = 1/2. When denominators are the same, add the numerators and simplify.

questions.text: What is 3/5 - 1/5?
hints: Same denominators means subtract the numerators directly.
questions.explanation: 3/5 - 1/5 = 2/5. Subtract the numerators when the denominators match.

hints: When adding fractions with the same denominator, do you add the denominators too?
Only the numerators are added; the denominator stays the same.
questions.explanation: 1/3 + 1/3 = 2/3, not 2/6. You add the numerators but keep the denominator the same.

questions.text: What is 2/3 x 3/4?
hints: Multiply numerator by numerator and denominator by denominator.
questions.explanation: 2/3 x 3/4 = 6/12 = 1/2. Multiply straight across and simplify.

questions.text: Simplify the fraction 4/8.
hints: Find the greatest common factor of 4 and 8.
questions.explanation: 4/8 simplified is 1/2. Divide both numerator and denominator by their GCF, which is 4.

questions.text: When multiplying two fractions, you multiply the numerators together and the denominators together.
hints: Think about how multiplication of fractions works.
There is no need for common denominators when multiplying.
questions.explanation: True. To multiply fractions, multiply numerator x numerator and denominator x denominator.

questions.text: What is 1/2 + 1/3?
hints: Find a common denominator for 2 and 3.
The LCD of 2 and 3 is 6. Convert both fractions.

questions.text: What is 5/6 - 1/3?
hints: Convert 1/3 so it has a denominator of 6.

questions.text: 3/4 is greater than 2/3.
hints: Convert both to the same denominator to compare.

questions.text: What is 1/5 x 5?
questions.explanation: 1/5 x 5 = 5/5 = 1. Any fraction multiplied by its denominator equals 1.

questions.text: What is 2/3 + 3/4?
A recipe calls for 2/3 cup of sugar. If you want to make half the recipe, how much sugar do you need?
What is 3/4 divided by 1/2?
hints: To divide by a fraction, multiply by its reciprocal.

questions.text: Sam ate 1/4 of a pizza and his sister ate 1/3. What fraction of the pizza did they eat altogether?
hints: Find a common denominator for 4 and 3.

questions.text: To divide by a fraction, you multiply by its reciprocal.
hints: Think about what 'Keep, Change, Flip' means.
The reciprocal of a/b is b/a.
questions.explanation: True. Dividing by a fraction is the same as multiplying by its reciprocal (flipped fraction).

questions.text: What is 5/6 x 3/10?
hints: Multiply numerators and denominators, then simplify.
questions.explanation: 5/6 x 3/10 = 15/60 = 1/4. You can also cross-cancel first.

questions.text: A ribbon is 7/8 of a meter long. If you cut off 1/4 of a meter, how much ribbon is left?
2/5 divided by 4 equals 2/20.
hints: Dividing by 4 is the same as multiplying by 1/4.

questions.text: In a math competition, a student must simplify (3/7 + 2/7) x 14. What is the answer?
hints: First add the fractions inside the parentheses.

questions.text: Find the value of 1/2 + 1/3 + 1/6.
A tank is 3/5 full. After using 1/3 of the water in the tank, what fraction of the full tank remains?
hints: 1/3 of 3/5 is used, so find 1/3 x 3/5 first.

questions.text: If you divide any fraction by itself, the result is always 1.
hints: Try it with an example like 3/4 divided by 3/4.
Any non-zero number divided by itself equals 1.
questions.explanation: True. Any non-zero number divided by itself equals 1. For example, 3/4 ÷ 3/4 = 3/4 x 4/3 = 12/12 = 1.

questions.text: If 2/3 of a number is 18, what is 5/6 of that number?
hints: First find the number: if 2/3 of it is 18, then the number is 18 ÷ 2/3.
The number is 27. Now find 5/6 of 27.

title: Prime Numbers & Factorization
description: Explore prime and composite numbers, find prime factorizations using factor trees, and apply divisibility rules.

questions.text: Which of the following is a prime number?
hints: A prime number has exactly 2 factors: 1 and itself.
Check if each number can be divided evenly by anything other than 1 and itself.
questions.explanation: 7 is prime because its only factors are 1 and 7. The others are composite: 9=3x3, 15=3x5, 21=3x7.

questions.text: The number 1 is a prime number.
hints: A prime must have exactly 2 distinct factors.
questions.explanation: False. The number 1 has only one factor (itself), so it is neither prime nor composite.

questions.text: What is the smallest prime number?
hints: Which is the first number with exactly two factors?
questions.explanation: 2 is the smallest prime number. It has exactly two factors: 1 and 2.

questions.text: Which of the following is a composite number?
hints: A composite number has more than 2 factors.
questions.explanation: 12 is composite because it has factors other than 1 and itself (2, 3, 4, 6). The rest are prime.

questions.text: All even numbers are composite.
hints: Is there an even number that is also prime?
questions.explanation: False. 2 is even and prime. It is the only even prime number.

questions.text: How many prime numbers are there between 1 and 10?
questions.explanation: The primes between 1 and 10 are 2, 3, 5, and 7. That is 4 prime numbers.

questions.text: What are the factors of 18?
hints: Try dividing 18 by each number from 1 to 18.
questions.explanation: The factors of 18 are 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, and 18. Each divides into 18 evenly.

questions.text: 15 is a prime number.
hints: Can 15 be divided evenly by any number other than 1 and 15?
questions.explanation: False. 15 = 3 x 5, so it is composite.

questions.text: What is the prime factorization of 12?
hints: Break 12 down into its smallest prime factors.
questions.explanation: 12 = 2 x 2 x 3. All factors are prime numbers.

questions.text: Which number is both even and prime?
hints: There is only one even prime number.
All other even numbers are divisible by 2 and another number.
questions.explanation: 2 is the only even prime number. Every other even number is divisible by 2 and at least one other number.

questions.text: What is the prime factorization of 36?
hints: Use a factor tree starting with 36.

questions.text: What is the greatest common factor (GCF) of 24 and 36?
hints: Find the prime factorization of both numbers.
24 = 2^3 x 3 and 36 = 2^2 x 3^2. Take the lower powers.

questions.text: The prime factorization of 30 is 2 x 3 x 5.
questions.explanation: True. 30 = 2 x 15 = 2 x 3 x 5. All factors are prime.

questions.text: How many prime numbers are there between 20 and 30?
hints: Which of those cannot be divided by anything except 1 and themselves?
questions.explanation: The primes between 20 and 30 are 23 and 29. That is 2 primes.

questions.text: What is the least common multiple (LCM) of 6 and 8?
hints: 6 = 2 x 3 and 8 = 2^3. Take the highest powers.

questions.text: A number is divisible by both 3 and 5. Which of these could it be?
hints: The number must be divisible by both 3 AND 5.
questions.explanation: 45 is divisible by 3 (45÷3=15) and by 5 (45÷5=9). 35 is not divisible by 3, 25 is not divisible by 3, and 33 is not divisible by 5.

questions.text: Every prime number greater than 2 is odd.
hints: If a number greater than 2 is even, it is divisible by 2.
That means it has more than 2 factors, so it cannot be prime.
questions.explanation: True. Any even number greater than 2 is divisible by 2 (and other factors), making it composite.

questions.text: What is the prime factorization of 60?
hints: Start by dividing 60 by the smallest prime.

questions.text: Which pair of numbers are both prime?
hints: Check each pair — both numbers must be prime.
questions.explanation: 11 and 13 are both prime (each has only 1 and itself as factors).

questions.text: The number 51 is prime.
hints: Add the digits: 5 + 1 = 6, which is divisible by 3.
questions.explanation: False. 51 = 3 x 17, so it is composite.

questions.text: In a competition, students are asked: what is the sum of all prime numbers less than 20? What is the answer?
Two numbers have a GCF of 6 and an LCM of 60. If one number is 12, what is the other?
hints: Use the property: GCF x LCM = product of the two numbers.
questions.explanation: GCF x LCM = a x b, so 6 x 60 = 360 = 12 x b, which gives b = 30.

questions.text: How many factors does 48 have?
If a number ends in 0 or 5, it is always composite.
hints: Numbers ending in 0 or 5 are divisible by 5.
questions.explanation: False. The number 5 ends in 5 and is prime. All other numbers ending in 0 or 5 are divisible by 5 and another number, making them composite.

questions.text: A number N is the product of three different prime numbers and N < 50. What is the largest possible value of N?
hints: Try combinations of three different primes whose product is less than 50.
2 x 3 x 7 = 42. Can you find a larger one under 50? 2 x 3 x 11 = 66, too big.
questions.explanation: Possible products: 2x3x5=30, 2x3x7=42, 2x5x7=70 (too big). The largest under 50 is 42 = 2 x 3 x 7.

title: Percentages
description: Understand percentages, convert between fractions, decimals, and percents, and solve real-world problems involving discounts, tax, and tips.

questions.text: What is 25% of 80?
Convert 1/2 to a percentage.
hints: To convert a fraction to a percent, multiply by 100.

questions.text: 50% is the same as 0.5.
hints: To convert percent to decimal, divide by 100.

questions.text: What is 10% of 200?
Convert 0.75 to a percentage.
hints: Multiply the decimal by 100 to get the percent.
questions.explanation: 0.75 x 100 = 75%. To convert a decimal to percent, multiply by 100.

questions.text: 100% of any number is the number itself.
hints: Multiplying any number by 1 gives the same number.
questions.explanation: True. 100% = 1, and any number times 1 equals itself.

questions.text: What percentage is 3 out of 12?
Convert 3/4 to a percentage.
20% of 50 is the same as 50% of 20.
questions.explanation: True. 20% of 50 = 10 and 50% of 20 = 10. Percentages are commutative in this way.

questions.text: What is 50% of 60?
A shirt costs $40 and is on sale for 15% off. What is the discount amount?
A student scored 18 out of 24 on a test. What is the percentage score?
If a price increases by 50% and then decreases by 50%, it returns to the original price.
hints: Try with $100: increase 50% to $150, then decrease 50%.
50% of $150 = $75, so new price is $75, not $100.
questions.explanation: False. Starting at $100: +50% = $150, then -50% = $75. The final price is less than the original.

questions.text: A book originally costs $25. After a 20% discount, what is the sale price?
If 30% of a number is 45, what is the number?
questions.topic: finding the whole from a percent
hints: Set up the equation: 0.30 x n = 45.

questions.text: A meal costs $35. If you want to leave a 15% tip, how much is the tip?
75% is equivalent to the fraction 3/4.
The population of a town grew from 5,000 to 6,000. What is the percent increase?
questions.explanation: Change = 1,000. Percent increase = (1000/5000) x 100 = 20%.

questions.text: An item costs $60 plus 8% sales tax. What is the total cost?
A 10% increase followed by a 10% decrease on a $200 item brings it back to $200.
A store offers 25% off, and then an additional 10% off the sale price. If the original price is $200, what is the final price?
In a class of 40 students, 60% are girls. If 5 more boys join the class, what percentage of the class is now girls?
hints: After 5 boys join: 24 girls out of 45 total.
questions.explanation: Girls = 60% of 40 = 24. New total = 45. Percentage of girls = 24/45 x 100 = 53.3%.

questions.text: A jacket's price was increased by 20% to $90. What was the original price?
hints: The increased price is 120% of the original.
questions.explanation: 120% of original = $90, so original = 90 ÷ 1.20 = $75.

questions.text: If you save 30% on a $50 item and 20% on a $80 item, your total savings are $31.
A toy is marked at $120. It is first discounted by 10%, and then sales tax of 5% is added. What is the final price?
hints: First find the discounted price: $120 x 0.90.
Then add 5% tax to the discounted price.

title: Linear Equations
description: Solve one-step and two-step linear equations, translate word problems into equations, and understand the concept of variables and equality.

questions.text: Solve for x: x + 7 = 12.
hints: To isolate x, subtract 7 from both sides.

questions.text: Solve for y: y - 3 = 10.
If x + 5 = 5, then x = 0.
Solve for x: 3x = 18.
Solve for n: n/4 = 5.
In the equation 2x = 10, x equals 20.
Solve for x: x - 15 = 0.
What value of x makes 5x = 35 true?
If x/3 = 9, then x = 27.
Solve for m: m + 25 = 40.
Solve for x: 2x + 3 = 11.
Solve for y: 3y - 7 = 14.
The solution to 4x - 8 = 0 is x = 2.
A number doubled and then increased by 5 gives 19. What is the number?
Solve for x: x/2 + 3 = 10.
Maria has some stickers. She gives away 8 and has 15 left. Which equation represents this situation?
hints: She starts with x stickers and gives away 8.
What she has left is x minus 8.
questions.explanation: She starts with x, gives away 8, and has 15 left: x - 8 = 15, so x = 23.

questions.text: The equation 5x + 10 = 35 has the solution x = 5.
Solve for x: 6x - 4 = 2x + 12.
questions.topic: equations with variables on both sides

questions.text: Three times a number minus 4 equals 17. What is the number?
If 2(x + 3) = 16, then x = 5.
A competition problem states: the sum of three consecutive integers is 72. What is the smallest of these integers?
hints: Let the integers be n, n+1, n+2.

questions.text: Tom is 4 years older than twice his sister's age. If Tom is 18, how old is his sister?
hints: Let sister's age = s. Tom's age = 2s + 4.
questions.explanation: 2s + 4 = 18 => 2s = 14 => s = 7. His sister is 7 years old.

questions.text: Solve for x: 3(2x - 1) + 4 = 25.
The equation 4(x - 2) = 2(x + 3) has the solution x = 7.
questions.topic: equations with parentheses on both sides

questions.text: A rectangular garden has a perimeter of 56 meters. If the length is 4 meters more than the width, what is the width?
hints: Perimeter = 2(length + width). Let width = w, length = w + 4.
questions.explanation: 2(w + w + 4) = 56 => 4w + 8 = 56 => 4w = 48 => w = 12 meters. Length = 16 meters.

title: Integers & Rational Numbers
description: Master integer operations, absolute value, and rational number concepts including addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of positive and negative numbers.

hints: When you add two negative numbers, you add their absolute values.
The result is always negative when both numbers are negative.
questions.explanation: Adding two negative numbers: add the absolute values (5 + 3 = 8) and keep the negative sign, giving -8.

questions.text: What is the absolute value of -12?
hints: Absolute value measures distance from zero on the number line.
questions.explanation: The absolute value of -12 is 12 because -12 is 12 units away from zero on the number line.

questions.text: The absolute value of any number is always positive or zero.
hints: Think about what absolute value represents on a number line.
Can a distance ever be negative?
questions.explanation: Absolute value represents distance from zero, and distance is always non-negative. So |x| >= 0 for all x.

questions.text: What is (-4) x (-6)?
hints: Multiplying two negative numbers gives what sign?
questions.explanation: A negative times a negative equals a positive: (-4) x (-6) = 24.

questions.text: What is 7 + (-10)?
hints: Adding a negative is the same as subtracting.
questions.explanation: 7 + (-10) = 7 - 10 = -3. Since 10 has a larger absolute value, the result is negative.

questions.text: The product of a positive integer and a negative integer is always negative.
hints: Think about the sign rules for multiplication.
Positive times negative gives what sign?
questions.explanation: When you multiply a positive number by a negative number, the result is always negative.

questions.text: Which fraction is equivalent to 0.75?
questions.explanation: 0.75 = 75/100 = 3/4 after dividing numerator and denominator by 25.

hints: Dividing two negative numbers gives what sign?
questions.explanation: A negative divided by a negative equals a positive: (-18) / (-3) = 6.

questions.text: Every integer is a rational number.
hints: A rational number can be written as a fraction a/b where b is not zero.
Can you write any integer as a fraction?
questions.explanation: Every integer n can be written as n/1, which is a fraction with integer numerator and non-zero integer denominator. So every integer is rational.

hints: First compute the sum inside the absolute value.
Then take the absolute value of the result.
The denominators are the same, so just add the numerators.
questions.explanation: Since the denominators match: (-2 + 1)/3 = -1/3.

questions.text: If the temperature drops from 5°F to -8°F, what is the total change in temperature?
questions.explanation: Change = -8 - 5 = -13°F. The temperature dropped 13 degrees.

hints: Multiply two at a time. (-3) x (-2) first.
An odd number of negative factors gives a negative product.
questions.explanation: (-3) x (-2) = 6, then 6 x (-4) = -24. With three negative factors (odd count), the product is negative.

questions.text: The number -7/4 is located between -2 and -1 on the number line.
questions.explanation: -7/4 = -1.75, and -2 < -1.75 < -1, so it lies between -2 and -1.

hints: Subtracting a negative is the same as adding.

questions.explanation: (-3/5) x (10/9) = -30/45 = -2/3 after dividing numerator and denominator by 15.

questions.text: If a and b are both negative integers, then a - b is always negative.
hints: Try a = -2, b = -5. What is a - b?
questions.explanation: If a = -2 and b = -5, then a - b = -2 - (-5) = -2 + 5 = 3, which is positive. So it is not always negative.

questions.text: A submarine is at -200 feet. It rises 75 feet, then descends 50 feet. What is its final depth?
hints: Rising means adding, descending means subtracting.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a rational number?
hints: A rational number can be written as a ratio of two integers.
Which of these cannot be expressed as a/b?
questions.explanation: sqrt(2) is irrational because it cannot be expressed as a fraction of two integers. The others are all rational.

questions.text: |a - b| is always equal to |b - a|.
hints: a - b and b - a are opposites of each other.
What does absolute value do to opposites?
questions.explanation: a - b = -(b - a), so |a - b| = |-(b - a)| = |b - a|. Absolute value of opposites are equal.

questions.text: If x is an integer and |x| = 7, what is the product of all possible values of x?
hints: If |x| = 7, what are the possible values of x?
questions.explanation: |x| = 7 means x = 7 or x = -7. Their product is 7 x (-7) = -49.

questions.text: The sum of three consecutive integers is -42. What is the largest of the three?
hints: Let the integers be n-1, n, n+1.
questions.explanation: Let the integers be n-1, n, n+1. Then 3n = -42, so n = -14. The largest is n+1 = -13.

questions.text: If a/b = -3/4 and b/c = 2/5, what is a/c?
For all integers a and b, |a + b| <= |a| + |b|.
hints: Try a few examples with positive and negative numbers.
questions.explanation: This is the triangle inequality. When a and b have opposite signs, cancellation makes |a + b| less than |a| + |b|. When same sign, they are equal.

questions.text: A stock price changes by -3, +5, -7, +2, and -1 over five days. What is the average daily change?
hints: Divide the total by the number of days.
questions.explanation: Total change = -3 + 5 + (-7) + 2 + (-1) = -4. Average = -4/5.

title: Pythagorean Theorem & Applications
description: Explore the Pythagorean theorem, identify Pythagorean triples, calculate distances on coordinate planes, and extend to 3D diagonal problems.

questions.text: In a right triangle with legs 3 and 4, what is the length of the hypotenuse?
The Pythagorean theorem only works for right triangles.
hints: Think about what makes the theorem special.
It relates the sides using the right angle.
questions.explanation: The Pythagorean theorem (a^2 + b^2 = c^2) applies only to right triangles, where c is the hypotenuse opposite the right angle.

questions.text: Which set of numbers is a Pythagorean triple?
In a right triangle, the hypotenuse is always the _____ side.
hints: The hypotenuse is opposite the largest angle.
The right angle (90°) is the largest angle in a right triangle.
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is opposite the right angle (the largest angle), making it always the longest side of a right triangle.

questions.text: A right triangle has legs of length 6 and 8. What is the hypotenuse?
hints: This is a multiple of the 3-4-5 triple.
questions.explanation: 6^2 + 8^2 = 36 + 64 = 100 = 10^2, so the hypotenuse is 10. This is 2 x (3, 4, 5).

questions.text: (6, 8, 10) is a Pythagorean triple.
questions.explanation: 6^2 + 8^2 = 36 + 64 = 100 = 10^2. Yes, (6, 8, 10) is a Pythagorean triple (it's 2 times the 3-4-5 triple).

questions.text: What is the distance between the points (0, 0) and (3, 4)?
hints: Use the distance formula or think of it as a right triangle.
The horizontal leg is 3 and the vertical leg is 4.

questions.text: A triangle with sides 7, 24, and 25 is a right triangle.
questions.explanation: 7^2 + 24^2 = 49 + 576 = 625 = 25^2. Since a^2 + b^2 = c^2, it is a right triangle.

questions.text: If a right triangle has a hypotenuse of 13 and one leg of 5, what is the other leg?
hints: Use a^2 + b^2 = c^2 and solve for the missing leg.

questions.text: The distance formula is derived from which theorem?
hints: The distance formula uses squares and a square root.
It treats the horizontal and vertical distances as legs.
questions.explanation: The distance formula d = sqrt((x2-x1)^2 + (y2-y1)^2) is a direct application of the Pythagorean theorem.

questions.text: A ladder 15 feet long leans against a wall. The base is 9 feet from the wall. How high up the wall does the ladder reach?
hints: The ladder is the hypotenuse, the distance from wall is one leg.

questions.text: What is the distance between points (1, 2) and (4, 6)?
If a triangle has sides 9, 12, and 15, then it is a right triangle.
questions.explanation: 9^2 + 12^2 = 81 + 144 = 225 = 15^2. This is 3 x (3, 4, 5), confirming it's a right triangle.

questions.text: A rectangular field is 40 meters by 30 meters. What is the diagonal distance across the field?
hints: The diagonal forms a right triangle with the sides.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a Pythagorean triple?
hints: Check each: does a^2 + b^2 = c^2?

questions.text: An isosceles right triangle has legs of length 1. What is the length of its hypotenuse?
If a^2 + b^2 > c^2 (where c is the longest side), the triangle is acute.
hints: For a right triangle, a^2 + b^2 = c^2 exactly.
If the sum of squares of the shorter sides exceeds the square of the longest side, the angle is less than 90°.
questions.explanation: When a^2 + b^2 > c^2, the angle opposite side c is less than 90°, making all angles acute.

questions.text: A right triangle has a hypotenuse of 10 and one leg of 6. What is the area of the triangle?
questions.explanation: Missing leg = sqrt(100 - 36) = sqrt(64) = 8. Area = (1/2)(6)(8) = 24.

questions.text: What is the distance between (-2, 3) and (4, -5)?
Every multiple of a Pythagorean triple is also a Pythagorean triple.
hints: If (a, b, c) is a triple, check (ka, kb, kc).

questions.text: A box is 3 feet long, 4 feet wide, and 12 feet tall. What is the length of the space diagonal (corner to opposite corner)?
hints: Use the 3D distance formula: d = sqrt(l^2 + w^2 + h^2).

questions.text: Two ships start at the same port. One sails 8 miles north, then 6 miles east. The other sails 15 miles south. How far apart are the two ships?
hints: Ship 1 is at (6, 8). Ship 2 is at (0, -15).
Find the distance between those two points.

questions.text: The diagonal of a square is 10 cm. What is the side length of the square?
hints: If the side is s, then s^2 + s^2 = 10^2.

questions.text: The 3D distance formula sqrt(a^2 + b^2 + c^2) can be derived by applying the Pythagorean theorem twice.
hints: First find the diagonal of the base: sqrt(a^2 + b^2).
Then use that as one leg and c as the other.
questions.explanation: First, d_base = sqrt(a^2 + b^2). Then d_space = sqrt(d_base^2 + c^2) = sqrt(a^2 + b^2 + c^2). This uses the theorem twice.

questions.text: A right triangle has an area of 30 and one leg of 5. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
hints: Area = (1/2)(5)(other leg) = 30. Find the other leg first.

title: Systems of Equations
description: Solve systems of linear equations using substitution, elimination, and graphical methods. Apply systems to real-world word problems.

questions.text: Solve the system: x + y = 10, x - y = 4. What is x?
hints: The y terms will cancel out.

questions.text: In the system x + y = 10, x - y = 4, what is y?
questions.explanation: x = 7, so 7 + y = 10, giving y = 3.

questions.text: A system of equations can have exactly one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions.
hints: Think about two lines on a graph.
They can intersect once, be parallel, or overlap.
questions.explanation: Two lines can intersect at one point (one solution), be parallel (no solution), or be the same line (infinitely many solutions).

questions.text: If y = 2x and x + y = 9, what is x?
hints: Substitute y = 2x into the second equation.
questions.explanation: Substituting: x + 2x = 9 → 3x = 9 → x = 3.

questions.text: Two lines that are parallel have how many solutions as a system?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never meet, so there are no solutions (the system is inconsistent).

questions.text: The solution to a system of two equations is the point where the two lines intersect on a graph.
hints: The solution satisfies both equations simultaneously.
questions.explanation: The intersection point is the (x, y) pair that satisfies both equations, which is the solution.

questions.text: Solve: y = x + 1 and y = 3. What is x?
hints: Since y = 3, substitute into the first equation.
questions.explanation: Substituting y = 3: 3 = x + 1, so x = 2.

questions.text: Which method involves adding or subtracting equations to eliminate a variable?
hints: This method combines equations to cancel one variable.
It's named after what it does to variables.
questions.explanation: The elimination method involves adding or subtracting equations to eliminate one variable.

questions.text: The system y = 2x + 1 and y = 2x + 5 has no solution.
hints: Both lines have the same slope.
Lines with equal slopes but different y-intercepts are parallel.
questions.explanation: Both lines have slope 2 but different y-intercepts (1 and 5), so they are parallel and never intersect.

questions.text: In the substitution method, the first step is to:

choices: Solve one equation for one variable
Set both equations equal to zero

hints: You need to express one variable in terms of the other.
Then you plug that expression into the other equation.
questions.explanation: In substitution, you first isolate one variable in one equation, then substitute that expression into the other equation.

questions.text: Solve by elimination: 2x + 3y = 12 and 2x - 3y = 0. What is x?
hints: Add the equations to eliminate y.
questions.explanation: Adding: 4x + 0y = 12, so x = 3.

questions.text: A movie ticket costs $8 and popcorn costs $5. Lisa spent $34 buying a total of 5 items. How many movie tickets did she buy?
hints: Let t = tickets and p = popcorn. t + p = 5 and 8t + 5p = 34.

questions.text: Solve: 3x + 2y = 16 and x - y = 2. What is y?
hints: From the second equation, x = y + 2.

questions.text: The system x + y = 5 and 2x + 2y = 10 has infinitely many solutions.
questions.explanation: Dividing the second equation by 2 gives x + y = 5, which is identical to the first. The lines overlap, giving infinitely many solutions.

questions.text: Solve: 4x - y = 10 and 2x + y = 8. What is the solution (x, y)?
hints: Add the equations to eliminate y.
questions.explanation: Adding: 6x = 18, x = 3. Then 2(3) + y = 8, y = 2. Solution: (3, 2).

questions.text: The sum of two numbers is 20 and their difference is 6. What are the two numbers?
questions.explanation: x + y = 20, x - y = 6. Adding: 2x = 26, x = 13. Then y = 7.

questions.text: If two equations have different slopes when written in slope-intercept form, the system has exactly one solution.
hints: Different slopes mean the lines are not parallel.
questions.explanation: Lines with different slopes are not parallel and must intersect at exactly one point, giving exactly one solution.

questions.text: Solve: 5x + 2y = 1 and 3x + 2y = -3. What is x?
hints: Subtract the second equation from the first.

questions.text: A parking lot has motorcycles (2 wheels) and cars (4 wheels). There are 10 vehicles and 28 wheels total. How many cars are there?
You can always use substitution to solve a system of two linear equations.
hints: You can always isolate one variable from one equation.
questions.explanation: Substitution always works for systems of linear equations, though sometimes elimination or graphing may be more efficient.

questions.text: Solve: x + 2y = 7 and 3x - 2y = 5. What is x?
hints: Add the two equations to eliminate y.
questions.explanation: Adding the equations: (x + 2y) + (3x - 2y) = 7 + 5 → 4x = 12 → x = 3. Then 3 + 2y = 7, so y = 2.

questions.text: A boat travels 30 miles upstream in 3 hours and 30 miles downstream in 2 hours. What is the speed of the boat in still water?
hints: Let b = boat speed and c = current speed.
Upstream: b - c = 30/3 = 10. Downstream: b + c = 30/2 = 15.
questions.explanation: b - c = 10 and b + c = 15. Adding: 2b = 25, b = 12.5 mph.

questions.text: For what value of k does the system x + 2y = 5 and 2x + 4y = k have infinitely many solutions?
hints: For infinitely many solutions, the equations must be equivalent.
questions.explanation: Multiplying eq1 by 2: 2x + 4y = 10. For the equations to be identical, k must equal 10.

questions.text: The system 2x + 3y = 7 and 4x + 6y = 15 has no solution.
questions.explanation: Multiplying eq1 by 2 gives 4x + 6y = 14, but eq2 says 4x + 6y = 15. Since 14 ≠ 15, the lines are parallel with no solution.

questions.text: At a school fair, adult tickets cost $6 and child tickets cost $3. If 200 tickets were sold for a total of $900, how many adult tickets were sold?
hints: From the first: c = 200 - a. Substitute.

title: Exponents & Scientific Notation
description: Master exponent rules including product, quotient, and power rules. Work with negative exponents and convert between standard and scientific notation.

questions.text: What is 2^3 x 2^4?
hints: When multiplying same bases, add the exponents.
questions.explanation: When multiplying powers with the same base, add exponents: 2^3 x 2^4 = 2^(3+4) = 2^7.

questions.text: What is (3^2)^3?
hints: Power of a power: multiply exponents.
questions.explanation: When raising a power to a power, multiply exponents: (3^2)^3 = 3^(2x3) = 3^6.
